# BUG-008 Writeup
> Generated by [Quality Playbook](https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook) v1.4.6 — Andrew Stellman
> Date: 2026-04-20 · Project: virtio-1.4.6

## 1. Summary

REQ-012 requires probe failure after `virtio_device_ready()` to reset the live device before deleting queues, but `drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c:321-339` reaches `DRIVER_OK` and then frees virtqueues without `virtio_reset_device(vdev)` if `input_register_device()` fails.

## 2. Spec reference

`formal_docs/writing_virtio_drivers.txt:121-130` ties `virtio_device_ready()` to queue enablement and transition into the live device-managed state, which means later teardown must restore the reset barrier before queue-owned state is released.

## 3. The code

`virtinput_probe()` calls `virtio_device_ready(vdev)` and sets `vi->ready = true` before `input_register_device()` (`drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c:321-325`). The `err_input_register` path only clears `ready` and falls through to `vdev->config->del_vqs(vdev)` (`:330-339`). In contrast, both `virtinput_remove()` and `virtinput_freeze()` call `virtio_reset_device(vdev)` before `del_vqs()` (`:349-358`, `:368-376`).

## 4. Observable consequence

A late probe failure can free queue-owned state after the transport has already been told the driver is ready. That makes the probe-failure branch the one teardown path in this driver that releases queues without first quiescing the live device, creating a teardown asymmetry right after `DRIVER_OK`.

## 5. The fix

Mirror the reset-before-del_vqs ordering already used by remove/freeze:

```diff
diff --git a/drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c b/drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c
index 8888888..9999999 100644
--- a/drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c
+++ b/drivers/virtio/virtio_input.c
@@ -330,6 +330,7 @@ static int virtinput_probe(struct virtio_device *vdev)
 err_input_register:
 	spin_lock_irqsave(&vi->lock, flags);
 	vi->ready = false;
 	spin_unlock_irqrestore(&vi->lock, flags);
+	virtio_reset_device(vdev);
 err_mt_init_slots:
 	input_free_device(vi->idev);
 err_input_alloc:
```

## 6. The test

- **Requirement:** REQ-012
- **Regression test:** `quality/test_regression.py::TestCodeReviewRegressions.test_bug_008_virtinput_probe_resets_before_del_vqs_after_ready`
- **Regression patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-008-regression-test.patch`
- **Fix patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-008-fix.patch`
- **Expected behavior:** the regression fails on the current tree because `virtio_reset_device(vdev)` is absent from the post-ready probe unwind, then passes once the fix patch adds that reset barrier.

## 7. FAIL -> PASS evidence

```text
RED: quality/results/BUG-008.red.log
GREEN: quality/results/BUG-008.green.log
```
